_action: DO NOT EDIT — update canonical source instead. See docs/SSOT/governance/GOVERNANCE_MAP.md

forbidden_patterns.description: Generic IRepository<T> or base repository classes. Logic goes directly in handlers using IDbSession.
IOrderService, IUserService etc. as intermediate layer between handler and data access. Handlers are the service.
BaseRepository<T> or CrudRepository<T> classes.
Any EF Core DbContext, IQueryable, migrations, or LINQ-to-SQL usage.
IdentityUser, UserManager<T>, SignInManager<T>, or any ASP.NET Identity scaffolding.
SQL written as C# string literals or interpolated strings inside handler or repository code.
Any pattern that generates SQL at runtime without an explicit .sql file (query builders, expression trees for SQL, etc.).
Implicit tenant or soft-delete filtering applied globally. All filters must be explicit in .sql files.
Any SQL query on a tenant table missing WHERE CustomerId = @CustomerId.
Injecting IHttpContextAccessor or HttpContext directly into MediatR handlers or Blazor components. Use ICurrentUser.
Throwing exceptions for expected business outcomes (not found, invalid input, auth failure). Use Result<T>.Fail instead.
Passing UserId, CustomerId, or ProfileId as raw int across feature boundaries. Use strongly-typed ID structs.
One feature directly importing or calling another feature's handler, command, or repository.
AutoMapper or any convention-based object mapping library.
Newtonsoft.Json (Json.NET). Use System.Text.Json exclusively.
A single .sql file used by more than one feature. One .sql file = one operation.
Static mutable fields or singleton services with mutable state across requests.
Any code that produces compiler warnings, nullability warnings, or analyzer warnings (including xUnit analyzers).

Dapper UPDATE statements that omit columns present in the target table. AI context limitations can cause silent field loss. Every UPDATE .sql file must SET all non-key, non-audit columns explicitly. Review the table schema before writing UPDATE SQL.
Using raw SqlConnection, IDbConnection, or SqlCommand directly instead of IDbSession. All DB access must go through IDbSession to ensure consistent connection lifecycle and testability.

Calling StateHasChanged() from a non-UI thread, or confusing Blazor Server component lifecycle (OnInitializedAsync, OnParametersSetAsync) with Blazor WASM patterns. Blazor Server has a persistent circuit — state mutations from background tasks must use InvokeAsync(() => StateHasChanged()).
